segments.text: The prettiest app often hide their worst flaw. Your thumb can't reach what matters. One-handed, your thumb takes a hit. Half of us scroll one-handed every
single day. But many apps still shove the key actions to the very extreme. Delete, stretch, save, stretch again.
It's like putting your car's steering wheel behind your head. The view will look sleek, but it's not going to work.
Good designers just flip the map. Bottom right, go. Bottom left, stop. And the top corners are only for the safe
things. See the difference? A button near your thumb feels natural. Move it away and it feels like a torture. Great design doesn't force you to stretch. It
stretches according to your needs. For more design content, subscribe.
